bio: I've guided teams and provided business solutions for organizations in over 16 countries, from startups to enterprises. My focus is on aligning people and processes into strategic, integrated solutions.

This experience shaped his vision for flexible, future-ready enterprise software. Colin holds a master's degree in computer information systems from Georgia State University. A passionate boater, fisherman and all-around winter enthusiast, he brings the same curiosity and drive that fuel his hobbies to his approach to innovation in technology.

Carlos Camacho: B.Sc. CS, Central University of Venezuela 2009; M.Sc. & Ph.D. CS, Complutense University of Madrid 2012 & 2017. Cum laude (formal SPL modeling). Research: formal methods, distributed systems, performance analysis, MLOps, AI & ML.

I have a broad range of experience across cloud native applications, cloud platforms and operations, domain-driven design, front-end and back-end architectures, and automation.  I've been helping folks tackle their hardest technical debt challenges for years, whether its modernizing legacy system, migrating to the cloud, or refactoring the organizational and team processes that get work done in a sustainable way.  At Moderne, we're building the factory to help you and your agents do this in a deterministic and repeatable way at enterprise scale.

jobTitle: Professor & Xu Bao Chancellor Chair, University of California, Berkeley

My work spans the full spectrum, from vector search and full-text indexing to RAG pipelines and agentic workflows. Before diving deep into data and AI, I wore many hats across Azure AI, Microsoft Mixed Reality, and Office 365, and spent five years at IBM Research developing ML models to understand health behavior and predict patient outcomes.

I hold a Ph.D. in Operations Research from UW-Madison and B.S. and M.Eng. degrees from Cornell University. That foundation in mathematical modeling and simulation still shapes how I think about systems, algorithms, and the problems worth solving. I've published 20+ papers, hold five patents, and regularly speak at major conferences on databases, search, RAG, and building with AI agents.

He is also a husband and father to 4yo twins and a newborn.  Taiki has been a fan of Power Platfform since its launch, and even has used it for his personal life – automating diaper orders for his kids, as well as proposing to his wife.

He has delivered business success in established products like Windows and Remote Desktop Services as well as in new initiatives like Azure Site Recovery. 
As a leader, Rochak has vast experience in building teams and driving collaboration across geographies. He believes a leader's role is to drive clarity, generate energy and care for the team. At Microsoft he has been recognized as top leader and consistently rated as top talent for his contributions and impact to the business.

Co-Founder and Hardware Lead of SpoilSafe, building IoT devices that help food distributors predict spoilage and cut waste. Leads hardware, firmware, and AI development, turning sensor data into actionable insights. ECE student at Carnegie Mellon.

I’m a student at Carnegie Mellon University studying Electrical and Computer Engineering and Robotics. I’m also the founder of SpoilSafe, where I build AI-driven solutions to reduce waste and improve decision-making in supply chains.

He specializes in building technical strategy and trusted relationships with partners including GitHub, NVIDIA, Arm, Inc., Oracle, IBM, Broadcom and Red Hat, while keeping up with competitors including AWS, Google, and emerging players like CoreWeave.

Vivek Chauhan leads the Fireworks fine tuning products portfolio, helping customer train open model to beat SOTA closed models on cost, speed, & quality. Over a 13-year career, he has shaped products across Financial services, AI, and data analytics.

David is a Cloud & AI Security Specialist, 6-time Microsoft Security MVP, with 10+ years securing startups, enterprises, and government. He has authored 6 books on cloud & AI security. He regularly speaks at industry events like Microsoft Ignite.

Ernesto is a Technology Architect and Microsoft MVP since 2017, specializing in cloud transformation, DevOps, serverless, and Generative AI. A frequent speaker at leading technology conferences, he helps organizations modernize business applications.

Anush Elangovan leads the Artificial Intelligence Group (AIG) as Corporate Vice President of AI software and solutions.
Anush has 23 years of industry experience in AI, computer science, compilers, network security, operating systems, math, and its materialization on complex hardware systems. This co-founder and Chief Executive Officer of Nod.ai oversaw product strategy and the overall business until AMD acquired Nod.ai (see related article here) today.
Anush will lead the acceleration of deploying AI solutions optimized for AMD products while aligning with AMD’s AI growth strategy centered on an open software ecosystem. In the near term, he and his team will introduce the code generation (CodeGen) capabilities from the Nod.ai flagship software, Shark, to unlock customer engagements via the ROCm™ and Vitis™ AI platforms. Over time, Anush will lead the contributions of the Nod.ai team to the AMD Unified AI Stack.
Before starting Nod.ai, Anush was instrumental in the graphics stack on the first ARM Chromebook. He led the movement of the Chrome operating system from Debian to Gentoo Linux to enable Google to gain full control of the shipping software. Previously, he was Principal Engineer for Agnilux, which Google acquired. The Agnilux team became crucial to the Chrome OS team, building a fusion of Android and Chrome OS.
Previously, Anush was a technical lead at Cisco Systems in its Datacenter Group, creating the first distributed virtual switching platform. He has also been an early member of FireEye, where he led in-memory taint-check analysis for networking and security in virtualized environments. He started his career in an earlier stint at Cisco, contributing to metro ethernet initiatives. Anush holds a Master of Science in computer science from Arizona State University and a Bachelor of Engineering in computer science from the Mepco Schlenk Engineering College at Madurai Kamaraj University in India. He has earned 10 patents. In his spare time, he enjoys skiing, mountaineering, and trail running. Anush lives with his family, including three children and two dogs, in the East Bay of the San Franciso Bay Area.

Edo Segal has spent over two decades building companies at the intersection of AI and human communication. As Chief Technology and Product Officer of Napster, he's overseeing the brand's shift from streaming music to streaming intelligence.

Satya Nadella is Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of Microsoft. Before being named CEO in February 2014, Nadella held leadership roles in both enterprise and consumer businesses across the company.
 
Joining Microsoft in 1992, he quickly became known as a leader who could span a breadth of technologies and businesses to transform some of Microsoft’s biggest product offerings.
 
Most recently, Nadella was executive vice president of Microsoft’s Cloud and Enterprise group. In this role he led the transformation to the cloud infrastructure and services business, which outperformed the market and took share from competition. Previously, Nadella led R&D for the Online Services Division and was vice president of the Microsoft Business Division. Before joining Microsoft, Nadella was a member of the technology staff at Sun Microsystems.
 
Originally from Hyderabad, India, Nadella lives in Bellevue, Washington, with his family. He earned a bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering from Mangalore University, a master’s degree in computer science from the University of Wisconsin – Milwaukee and a master’s degree in business administration from the University of Chicago. Nadella serves on the board of trustees to his alma mater the University of Chicago.

jobTitle: Chief Technology Officer, Deputy CISO, and Technical Fellow, Microsoft Azure
bio: Mark Russinovich is CTO, Deputy CISO, and Technical Fellow, Microsoft Azure, Microsoft’s global enterprise-grade cloud platform. A widely recognized expert in distributed systems, operating systems and cybersecurity, Mark earned a Ph.D. in computer engineering from Carnegie Mellon University. He later co-founded Winternals Software, joining Microsoft in 2006 when the company was acquired. Mark is a popular speaker at industry conferences such as Microsoft Ignite, Microsoft Build, and RSA Conference. He has authored several nonfiction and fiction books, including the Microsoft Press Windows Internals book series, Troubleshooting with the Sysinternals Tools, as well as fictional cyber security thrillers Zero Day, Trojan Horse and Rogue Code.
building the latest and greatest app dev experiences on Fabric - passionate about javascript, databases, and developer experience.

I build and lead high-touch customer programs that connect enterprises with the people and insights needed to drive better decisions—while helping technology companies do the same in return. My work sits at the intersection of customer engagement, platform strategy, and cross-functional execution. I specialize in creating new functions from the ground up, aligning commercial leadership with customer needs, and operating effectively in complex, ambiguous environments where influence must be earned.
